text: Plausibility is high. CYP19a1 aromatase is rate-limiting for the  synthesis of E2 in vertebrates (Simpson et al. 1994; Payne et al. 2004), so inhibition of the enzyme reduces E2 levels. Gonadal differentiation of many non-mammalian vertebrates, including a number of fish species, is dependent upon signaling associated with the sex steroids T and E2 (Guiguen et al. 2010; Nakamura 2010). In many of these species there exists a bipotential gonad during early development that, based on steroidal signaling, can differentiate into either testis of ovary.  When the "default" differentiation pathway is to testis, as is often the case (Angelopoulou et al. 2012), decreases in E2 plausibly favor the development of testis.
